expectations.text: Dockerfile installs .NET SDK 8 (dotnet-sdk-8.0 or equivalent) alongside Node.js
expectations.evidence: Dockerfile.dev line 20 installs dotnet-sdk-8.0 via Microsoft apt feed, on top of FROM node:20.11.1-bookworm

expectations.text: Dockerfile does NOT bake in COPY package*.json + npm install (would break fresh-workspace init)
expectations.evidence: Dockerfile.dev lines 30-35 explicitly comment that npm install is NOT done at image build, with rationale; no COPY package.json present

expectations.text: Plan runs create-nx-workspace INSIDE a docker container, not on the host
expectations.evidence: PLAN.md step 2 uses `docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.init.yml run --rm nx-init npx create-nx-workspace@latest .`
PLAN.md step 2: `--preset=angular-monorepo` with explicit note that angular-standalone is wrong
PLAN.md step 3 uses `nx add @nx/dotnet` and explicitly contrasts with npm install

expectations.text: Plan creates a sample .NET webapi via `dotnet new webapi` inside the container
expectations.evidence: PLAN.md step 4: `dotnet new webapi -o ./apps/pollen-api` via the init compose service

expectations.text: Plan runs `nx configure-ai-agents` (or equivalent) to wire up Claude/Nx MCP
expectations.evidence: PLAN.md step 5 runs `npx nx configure-ai-agents` and explains it generates CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md and the Nx MCP server

expectations.text: Angular serve command in compose includes --host 0.0.0.0
CHOKIDAR_USEPOLLING=true is set in serve compose file
Port 4200 is published from serve container to the host
Assistant does NOT recommend installing Node or .NET SDK on the host
expectations.evidence: PLAN.md opening: 'All commands run inside Docker — you do not need Node or the .NET SDK on your Mac.' Every step uses `docker compose run` wrapping
